channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: What to do in Brussels this weekend: 19 - 21 June
item.description: With the summer solstice arriving this weekend, Brussels is making the most of the season's longest days. Here are our favourite picks!

item.title: Everything you need to know about Fête de la Musique
item.description: On any given weekend in Brussels, live music isn't hard to find. But this weekend, it's everywhere.

item.title: An unusual walking tour through Brussels – Europe’s capital of exiles
item.description: Asylum has always been central to the identity of Belgium as an independent nation and to Brussels as the national and European capital.

item.title: Teenagers in Belgium most likely to grow up in workless households in EU
item.description: Belgium's teenagers are the most likely in the EU to grow up in households where adults barely work, according to figures published by Eurostat.

item.title: Partial dismantling of Brussels' historic Palais du Midi to officially go ahead
item.description: The Brussels Region has authorised the controversial dismantling of the heritage building to continue work on the stalled Metro 3 project.

item.title: Brussels wants new drug czar to tackle 'hardest problem city has faced since terror attacks'
item.description: The Brussels Minister-President confirmed there will be a drug commissioner after all amid a spate of drug-related explosions in Brussels.

item.title: European Citizen Action Service Day takes place in Brussels next week
item.description: On the agenda are a keynote speech by European Commissioner for Democracy Michael McGrath, several panel discussions, thematic sessions and workshops.

item.title: Threats against Securail staff, Brussels opens investigation
item.description: "The safety of our staff is our top priority."

item.title: Brussels for kids: What to do with your family this weekend – 19 to 21 June
item.description: From concerts in the park to puppet shows and midsummer garland workshops, there are plenty of family-friendly activities taking place this weekend.

item.title: Traffic disruptions expected in Brussels due to EU summit
item.description: The two-day summit will result in major traffic disruptions in the Belgian capital. Here's what you need to know.

item.title: Police appeal for witnesses after violent assault in Saint-Gilles square
item.description: A young man was attacked by a gang of assailants.

item.title: EU urged to establish uniform framework for shared e-scooters
item.description: The call comes less than a week after Brussels decided to ban shared e-scooters from its streets.

item.title: Legendary Brussels restaurant celebrates ‘extraordinary milestone’ with €750 'prestige' menu
item.description: The Michelin-starred restaurant is celebrating its 100th anniversary with special menu.

item.title: Court convicts former ULB student of assault but dismisses antisemitic motive
item.description: A former student at ULB faced charges for assaulting two individuals during the first day of a pro-Palestinian student occupation of a university building.

item.title: Mont des Arts to become open-air stage during July arts festival
item.description: Arts at Night will take place on Friday, 3 July, offering exhibitions, performances, live music, and DJ sets after sunset.

item.title: STIB set to scrap Floya transport app following scooter ban in Brussels
item.description: Floya aimed to simplify the use of multiple modes of transport through a single platform.

item.title: Brussels cracks down on oversized lorries entering city's tunnels
item.description: Vehicles exceeding 3.5 tonnes are prohibited in the city’s tunnels due to safety and infrastructure concerns.

item.title: Changing your habits: Practical tips for reducing the spread of microplastics
item.description: From driving less to ditching plastic bottles, Belgian health authorities say there are lots of things individuals can do to help protect the environment from the scourge of microplastics.

item.title: Why a record number of people became Belgian in 2025
item.description: Aside from a blip in 2020, the number of people becoming Belgian nationals each year has been steadily climbing since 2014.

item.title: Belgium v Egypt: Where to watch the Red Devils in Brussels tonight
item.description: Whether it's in a packed fan village, a neighbourhood sports hall or a local bar, Brussels looks set to spend tonight's game united by a familiar refrain: "Allez les Diables."
